title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the average acceleration of the shell in the tube, we need to know the initial and final velocities and the distance over which the acceleration occurs.

title: Initial and Final Velocities
text: The initial velocity of the shell is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ since it starts from rest. We need to find the final velocity as it leaves the tube.

title: Using Kinematic Equations
text: Use the kinematic equation v² $$=$$ u² + 2as to find the final velocity, where v is the final velocity, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the distance.

title: Calculate Final Velocity
text: Given that the shell reaches a height of $$110.0$$ $$m$$, use the kinematic equation to find the velocity as it leaves the tube.

title: Finding Average Acceleration
text: Now that you have the final velocity, use the formula a $$=$$ $$\frac{v-u}{t}$$ to find the average acceleration, where $$t$$ is the time taken to travel the length of the tube.

title: Calculate Average Acceleration
text: Use the formula a $$=$$ (v²-u²)/(2s) to find the average acceleration, where s is the length of the tube.
